description: AI-powered math research VSCode extension with branching dialogue, RAG knowledge retrieval, and paper writing

stories.title: Extension Scaffold and Activation
stories.description: As a developer, I want the VS Code extension project scaffolded with proper structure so that all subsequent features have a foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Extension activates without errors when installed in VS Code
package.json declares extension metadata, activation events, and contribution points
src/extension.ts contains activate() and deactivate() functions
Project builds with npm run compile without errors
Extension contributes a command mathAgent.openPanel visible in command palette
Typecheck/lint passes

tasks.title: Initialize project with package.json and tsconfig
tasks.description: Create package.json with: name 'math-research-agent', displayName 'Math Research Agent', publisher field, engines.vscode '^1.100.0', main './out/extension.js'. Add activationEvents: ['onCommand:mathAgent.openPanel', 'onChatParticipant:math-research-agent.math']. Add contributes.commands with mathAgent.openPanel (title: 'Open Math Research Studio'). Create tsconfig.json targeting ES2022, module commonjs, outDir ./out, rootDir ./src, strict true. Add .eslintrc.json with @typescript-eslint. Add scripts: compile (tsc -p ./), watch (tsc -watch -p ./), lint (eslint src --ext ts). Install devDependencies: typescript, @types/vscode, @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin, @typescript-eslint/parser, eslint. Create .vscodeignore with standard entries.

tasks.title: Create extension entry point and directory structure
tasks.description: Create src/extension.ts with activate(context: vscode.ExtensionContext) and deactivate() functions. In activate, register a command 'mathAgent.openPanel' that shows an info message 'Math Research Studio (placeholder)'. Create src/services.ts with export function createServices(context: ExtensionContext) that returns a typed object of all services (initially empty, each story will add its service). activate() calls createServices and stores result. Create empty directories: src/llm/, src/dialogue/, src/knowledge/, src/persona/, src/lean4/, src/chat/, src/webview/, src/webview/handlers/, src/papers/, src/utils/, src/test/. Add a .gitkeep in each empty dir.

stories.title: LLM Service — Multi-Provider Abstraction
stories.description: As a user, I want to connect my preferred LLM provider so that I can use GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, or VS Code Copilot models.
acceptanceCriteria: LlmService exposes a unified sendMessage(messages, options): AsyncIterable<string> interface
Supports four providers: vscode-lm, openai, anthropic, google
Provider selection configured via mathAgent.llm.provider setting
Model name configured per provider via mathAgent.llm.openaiModel, mathAgent.llm.anthropicModel, mathAgent.llm.googleModel settings
API keys stored in VS Code SecretStorage, never in plaintext settings
When API key is missing or invalid, throws a typed LlmAuthError with provider name
When provider API returns a rate limit error, throws a typed LlmRateLimitError
Streaming responses yield string chunks as they arrive
Unit tests cover: provider switching, auth error handling, rate limit error handling
Typecheck/lint passes

tasks.title: Define LLM types and provider interface

Export all types.

Add test script to package.json: 'test:unit': 'mocha --require ts-node/register src/test/**/*.test.ts'. Install ts-node as devDependency.

stories.title: First-Run Onboarding Wizard
stories.description: As a new user, I want a guided setup wizard so that I can configure my LLM provider without reading documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: On first activation (no provider configured and no API keys stored), a multi-step QuickPick wizard launches
Step 1: Choose your LLM provider — shows four options: VS Code Copilot, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google
Step 2 (if not VS Code Copilot): Enter your API key — secure input box with password masking
Step 3: Test connection — sends a simple test prompt, shows success or failure message
On success: stores API key in SecretStorage, sets mathAgent.llm.provider in settings, shows Ready! notification
On failure: shows error message with Try again and Skip options
Wizard does not re-launch once a provider is configured
Typecheck/lint passes

tasks.title: Implement onboarding wizard function
tasks.description: Create src/onboarding.ts. Export async function runOnboardingWizard(llmService: LlmService, context: ExtensionContext). Step 1: vscode.window.showQuickPick with items [{label:'VS Code Copilot', id:'vscode-lm'}, {label:'OpenAI (GPT-5.4)', id:'openai'}, {label:'Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.6)', id:'anthropic'}, {label:'Google (Gemini 3.1 Pro)', id:'google'}]. If user cancels, return. Step 2: if not vscode-lm, show vscode.window.showInputBox with password:true, prompt 'Enter your API key'. Store key in SecretStorage with appropriate key name. Step 3: call llmService.setProvider(chosen), try llmService.sendMessage with test prompt 'Hello', collect first chunk. On success: update mathAgent.llm.provider config, show info message 'Ready!'. On failure: show error with 'Try again'/'Skip' options; if Try again, recurse. Set context.globalState 'mathAgent.onboardingComplete' = true.

Also register a command 'mathAgent.configureProvider' that always runs the wizard (for re-configuration). Add the command to package.json contributes.commands.

stories.title: Dialogue Tree Data Model and Tree Manager
stories.description: As a developer, I want a tree data structure and manager so that branching conversations can be created, navigated, and persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: DialogueNode interface: id, parentId, role ('user'|'assistant'), content, children (string[]), metadata ({ timestamp, model, persona?, sources?, lean4Result? })
DialogueTree interface: id, title, rootId, activePath (string[]), nodes (Record<string, DialogueNode>), createdAt, updatedAt

TreeManager class with methods: createTree(title), addNode(treeId, parentId, role, content, metadata), forkFrom(treeId, nodeId), switchBranch(treeId, nodeId), deleteBranch(treeId, nodeId), getActivePath(treeId)
forkFrom creates a new user node as a child of the specified node and updates activePath
deleteBranch removes the target node and all its descendants from nodes, and removes the target from its parent's children array
getActivePath returns an array of DialogueNode objects in order from root to the deepest node along activePath
Unit tests cover: create tree, add nodes, fork, switch branch, delete branch with children, get active path
Typecheck/lint passes

tasks.title: Define dialogue tree types
tasks.description: Create src/dialogue/types.ts. Define and export: NodeMetadata interface ({timestamp: number, model: string, persona?: string, sources?: Citation[], lean4Result?: Lean4Result}), DialogueNode interface ({id: string, parentId: string|null, role: 'user'|'assistant', content: string, children: string[], metadata: NodeMetadata}), DialogueTree interface ({id: string, title: string, rootId: string, activePath: string[], nodes: Record<string, DialogueNode>, createdAt: number, updatedAt: number, attachedPapers?: AttachedPaper[]}). Import Citation from src/knowledge/types.ts (create stub), Lean4Result from src/lean4/types.ts (create stub), AttachedPaper from src/dialogue/types.ts (define inline). Define AttachedPaper: {id: string, source: 'arxiv'|'local-pdf'|'local-tex', title: string, arxivId?: string, filePath?: string, extractedText: string, selectedSections?: string[], scope: 'global'|'branch', branchId?: string}.

tasks.title: Write tests for TreeManager
tasks.description: Create src/test/dialogue/tree.test.ts. Tests: (1) createTree returns a tree with a root node, empty activePath, and given title, (2) addNode creates a node with correct parentId and adds it to parent's children, (3) addNode to root sets activePath to [rootId, newNodeId], (4) forkFrom creates a new child on the specified node, updates activePath to path through new child, (5) switchBranch updates activePath to follow the specified node's lineage from root, (6) deleteBranch removes node and all descendants, removes from parent's children, (7) deleteBranch on active path switches activePath to sibling or parent, (8) getActivePath returns ordered nodes from root to leaf, (9) boundary: addNode with null parentId throws, (10) boundary: deleteBranch on root throws, (11) boundary: switchBranch with invalid nodeId throws.

tasks.title: Implement TreeManager
tasks.description: Create src/dialogue/tree.ts. Class TreeManager. Private trees: Map<string, DialogueTree>. createTree(title): generate UUID for tree and root node, create root DialogueNode with role 'system', content 'Tree created', empty children. Return tree. addNode(treeId, parentId, role, content, metadata): generate UUID, create node, add to parent.children, update activePath by appending new node. forkFrom(treeId, nodeId): find node, recalculate activePath as path from root to nodeId (the fork point). User will add a message after forking. switchBranch(treeId, nodeId): walk from nodeId up to root to build path, then walk from nodeId down to deepest leaf following first child. Set as activePath. deleteBranch(treeId, nodeId): recursively collect all descendant IDs, delete from nodes map, remove from parent.children. If nodeId is in activePath, switch to nearest sibling or parent. getActivePath(treeId): map activePath IDs to nodes. Install uuid, @types/uuid. Pass all tests.

stories.title: Storage Service — Tree Persistence
stories.description: As a user, I want my dialogue trees saved to disk so that I can resume research sessions across VS Code restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: StorageService saves dialogue trees as JSON to .math-agent/trees/{treeId}.json in the workspace root
Auto-creates .math-agent/trees/ directory if it doesn't exist
On every save, creates a .backup copy of the previous version at .math-agent/trees/{treeId}.backup.json
loadTree(treeId) reads and parses the JSON file, returning a DialogueTree
listTrees() returns metadata (id, title, updatedAt) for all trees in the directory
When JSON parsing fails, throws a typed CorruptTreeError with the tree ID
.math-agent/ is added to .gitignore if a .gitignore exists in the workspace
Unit tests cover: save/load round-trip, backup creation, corrupt file detection, list trees
Typecheck/lint passes

tasks.title: Write tests for StorageService
tasks.description: Create src/test/dialogue/storage.test.ts. Use a temp directory for test workspace. Tests: (1) saveTree creates .math-agent/trees/{id}.json with valid JSON, (2) saveTree creates .backup.json on second save, (3) loadTree returns parsed DialogueTree matching saved data, (4) loadTree with corrupt JSON throws CorruptTreeError, (5) loadTree with nonexistent file throws error, (6) listTrees returns array of {id, title, updatedAt} for all tree files, (7) listTrees on empty directory returns empty array, (8) saveTree auto-creates .math-agent/trees/ directory. Use fs.mkdtemp for temp dirs, cleanup in afterEach.

tasks.title: Implement StorageService
tasks.description: Create src/dialogue/storage.ts. Class StorageService. Constructor takes workspaceRoot: string. Private getTreeDir(): path.join(workspaceRoot, '.math-agent', 'trees'). saveTree(tree): ensure dir exists (fs.mkdirSync recursive), if file exists copy to .backup.json, write JSON.stringify(tree, null, 2) to {treeId}.json, update tree.updatedAt. loadTree(treeId): read file, try JSON.parse, catch and throw CorruptTreeError(treeId). listTrees(): readdir, filter .json (not .backup), parse each for {id, title, updatedAt} (use a lightweight read — just parse enough for metadata). Define CorruptTreeError class extending Error. Add ensureGitignore(): if .gitignore exists in workspace, check if '.math-agent/' is in it, if not append. Call ensureGitignore in saveTree. Pass all tests.

stories.title: Context Builder
stories.description: As a developer, I want a service that assembles LLM context from the active branch path, attached papers, and RAG results, so that each branch gets coherent context.
acceptanceCriteria: ContextBuilder.build(tree, nodeId, options) returns an array of LLM messages in order: system prompt, attached papers context, path messages (root to nodeId), RAG context, user message
System prompt is determined by the active persona configuration
Path messages are extracted by walking from root to the specified node along parent links
Attached papers are injected as a system message with paper title and extracted text
RAG results (if any) are injected as a system message with source attribution
No messages from sibling branches are included (no cross-contamination)
Unit tests cover: basic path assembly, persona system prompt injection, attached paper inclusion, RAG inclusion, sibling exclusion
Typecheck/lint passes

tasks.title: Write tests for ContextBuilder
tasks.description: Create src/test/dialogue/context.test.ts. Build test trees with known structures (linear path, branching). Tests: (1) build() returns messages starting with system prompt from persona, (2) path messages are in order from root to target node (excluding root system node), (3) sibling branch messages are NOT included, (4) when attachedPapers provided with global scope, a system message with paper content appears after system prompt, (5) when RAG citations provided in options, a system message with formatted citations appears before user message, (6) when no persona specified, uses a default math assistant system prompt, (7) branch-scoped attached paper is included only when building context for that branch.

tasks.title: Implement ContextBuilder
tasks.description: Create src/dialogue/context.ts. Class ContextBuilder. Constructor takes PersonaManager ref. build(tree, nodeId, options?: {persona?: string, ragCitations?: Citation[], maxTokens?: number}): (1) Get persona system prompt from PersonaManager (or default), create system message. (2) Collect attached papers: filter tree.attachedPapers for scope='global' or scope='branch' matching current branch. Create system message with each paper's title and extractedText. (3) Walk from root to nodeId: start at nodeId, follow parentId links to root, reverse the array. Map to LlmMessage objects (skip root system node). (4) If ragCitations provided, create system message: 'Reference material from sources:\n' + formatted citations. (5) Return assembled array. Import LlmMessage from llm/types. Pass all tests.

stories.title: Context Trimming and Memory Persistence
stories.description: As a user with deep research sessions, I want the system to handle context window limits gracefully while preserving a memory file of trimmed content.
acceptanceCriteria: ContextBuilder accepts a maxTokens parameter (estimated via character count / 4 approximation)
When assembled context exceeds maxTokens, trimming order is: (1) RAG results removed first, (2) older path messages summarized, (3) attached papers preserved last
When trimming occurs, a memory file is written to .math-agent/trees/{treeId}/memory/context-trim-{timestamp}.md
Memory file Part 1: structured summary with headings Main Idea, Current Tasks, Key Results
Memory file Part 2: full transcript of the trimmed messages under heading Full Transcript
The summary in Part 1 is generated by sending the trimmed messages to the LLM with a summarization prompt
Unit tests cover: trimming order, memory file creation, memory file format
Typecheck/lint passes

tasks.title: Write tests for context trimming
tasks.description: Create src/test/dialogue/context-trimming.test.ts. Tests: (1) when total chars/4 < maxTokens, no trimming occurs and all messages returned, (2) when exceeding maxTokens, RAG system message is removed first, (3) after RAG removal if still exceeding, oldest path messages are replaced with a summary message, (4) attached paper messages are never removed (trimming stops before them), (5) estimateTokens(messages) returns sum of chars/4 for all message contents, (6) when trimming occurs, writeMemoryFile is called with correct path and content format. Mock LLM for summarization and fs for file writing.

tasks.title: Implement trimming in ContextBuilder
tasks.description: Modify src/dialogue/context.ts. Add to ContextBuilder: private estimateTokens(messages: LlmMessage[]): number (sum content.length/4). In build(), after assembling all messages, if options.maxTokens is set and estimateTokens > maxTokens: (1) Remove RAG system message, recheck. (2) If still over, identify path messages (not system/paper), take oldest N until under budget, replace with a single summary placeholder message '[Earlier context summarized — see memory file]'. (3) Call writeTrimMemory(treeId, trimmedMessages, llmService). writeTrimMemory: create .math-agent/trees/{treeId}/memory/ dir, call LLM with 'Summarize this conversation focusing on: Main Idea, Current Tasks, Key Results', write .md file with Part 1 (LLM summary) and Part 2 (full transcript of trimmed messages joined with newlines). Pass all tests.

stories.title: Math Persona Manager

Custom personas can be defined in mathAgent.customPersonas setting as JSON array of PersonaConfig objects

stories.title: Knowledge Service — arXiv Client
stories.description: As a researcher, I want to search arXiv for papers so that I can find relevant literature during exploration.
acceptanceCriteria: ArxivClient.search(query, maxResults) sends HTTP GET to https://export.arxiv.org/api/query with search_query and max_results params
Parses Atom XML response into an array of Citation objects with: source 'arxiv', title, url (abs link), snippet (abstract truncated to 500 chars), bibtex (auto-generated from metadata), fetchedAt
BibTeX generation includes: @article{arxivId, title, author, year, journal={arXiv preprint}, eprint={arxivId}}
Respects rate limiting: maximum 1 request per 3 seconds (queued)
Results are cached in KnowledgeCache with 24-hour TTL
When arXiv API returns non-200 status, returns empty array and logs warning (does not throw)
Unit tests with fixture XML responses cover: parsing, BibTeX generation, caching, error handling
Typecheck/lint passes

tasks.title: Create arXiv fixture data and write tests
tasks.description: Create src/test/knowledge/fixtures/arxiv-response.xml with a sample Atom XML response containing 2 entries (copy realistic structure from arXiv API docs with entry, title, summary, author, id, published fields). Create src/test/knowledge/arxiv.test.ts. tasks.description: Create src/webview/protocol.ts. Define discriminated union types. WebviewToHost: {type:'send', content:string, nodeId?:string} | {type:'fork', nodeId:string} | {type:'deleteBranch', nodeId:string} | {type:'switchBranch', nodeId:string} | {type:'stopStream'} | {type:'setPersona', personaId:string} | {type:'setModel', provider:string, model:string} | {type:'requestState'} | {type:'addPaper'} | {type:'verifyLean4', nodeId:string} | {type:'retryLean4', nodeId:string, attempt:number} | {type:'getTexFiles'} | {type:'selectTexFile', filePath:string} | {type:'draftFromBranch', branchNodeId:string} | {type:'insertIntoFile', filePath:string, afterLine:number, content:string} | {type:'listTrees'} | {type:'selectTree', treeId:string} | {type:'createTree', title:string} | {type:'renameTree', treeId:string, title:string} | {type:'deleteTree', treeId:string}. HostToWebview: {type:'treeState', tree:DialogueTree} | {type:'streamChunk', nodeId:string, text:string} | {type:'streamEnd', nodeId:string} | {type:'ragStatus', nodeId:string, status:RagStatus} | {type:'personas', personas:PersonaConfig[]} | {type:'providers', providers:Array<{id:string,model:string}>} | {type:'multiAgentResult', responses:any[], synthesis:string} | {type:'lean4Result', nodeId:string, result:any} | {type:'treeList', trees:any[]} | {type:'texFiles', files:any[]} | {type:'texStructure', structure:any[]} | {type:'draftResult', latex:string}. Create src/webview/message-handler.ts: export type MessageHandler = (msg: WebviewToHost, panel: MathResearchPanel) => Promise<void>. Export class MessageHandlerRegistry with private handlers: Map<string, MessageHandler>. Methods: register(type: string, handler: MessageHandler), handle(msg: WebviewToHost, panel): look up handler by msg.type, call it, log warning if unknown type. This pattern ensures panel.ts stays thin — each feature registers its own handler. Also create webview-ui/src/types.ts mirroring the HostToWebview types.

On non-zero exit: parse stderr into diagnostics array (split by newline), return {status:'error',...}. On timeout: kill process, return {status:'timeout',...}. Clean up temp file in finally block. progress.details: Scaffolded VS Code extension with package.json, tsconfig, eslint flat config, extension.ts, services.ts, and directory structure
progress.learnings: ESLint v9 requires flat config (eslint.config.mjs), not .eslintrc.json

progress.details: Implemented webview panel shell with React build pipeline (esbuild), tab navigation (Research/Write), MathResearchPanel provider with CSP, and wired openPanel command
progress.learnings: esbuild bundles React webview-ui with IIFE format for VS Code webviews. CSP requires nonce for scripts, unsafe-inline for styles with webview.cspSource. webview-ui is excluded from main tsconfig.json and has its own tsconfig targeting ES2020 with jsx react-jsx.

progress.details: Webview panel with React build pipeline (esbuild), App with tab navigation (Research/Write), MathResearchPanel class with CSP and nonce
progress.learnings: esbuild IIFE format works well for VS Code webviews. Nonce-based CSP for security.

progress.details: PersonaManager with 7 built-in personas (algebraist, analyst, geometer, topologist, number-theorist, logician, multi-agent), custom persona support via settings, dependency-injected config getter for testability
progress.learnings: Node 24 with tsx works well for mocha test runner. Dependency injection via ConfigGetter avoids need to mock vscode module. cross-env needed for Windows npm scripts with env vars.

progress.details: Dialogue tree data model (types for DialogueNode, DialogueTree, NodeMetadata, AttachedPaper) and TreeManager class with create, add, fork, switch, delete, getActivePath, loadTree methods. Stub types for Citation and Lean4Result. 17 unit tests covering all methods and boundary cases.
progress.learnings: TypeScript strict mode requires explicit type annotations on variables inside while loops that reference Record<string, T> lookups to avoid TS7022 circular implicit type errors. tsx loader works better than ts-node/register for mocha on Node 24.

progress.details: PersonaManager with 7 built-in personas (algebraist, analyst, geometer, topologist, number-theorist, logician, multi-agent). 13 tests passing. ConfigGetter injection for testability.
progress.learnings: Dependency-injected ConfigGetter pattern works well for testability without mocking vscode module.

progress.details: LLM Service multi-provider abstraction: LlmMessage/LlmOptions/LlmProvider interfaces, LlmAuthError/LlmRateLimitError error classes, LlmService class with provider registration/switching/sendMessage delegation and SecretStorage-based API key management. 11 unit tests passing. VS Code settings for provider selection and model configuration.
progress.learnings: Mocha v11 uses ESM import() for test files. On Node 24, use module.register() with custom resolve hook in .mjs file loaded via --import to mock vscode module. Compile-then-run (tsc + mocha on out/) is more reliable than ts-node/tsx for ESM+CJS interop in VS Code extension tests.

progress.details: TreeManager with createTree, addNode, forkFrom, switchBranch, deleteBranch, getActivePath. 17 tests passing. Types for DialogueNode, DialogueTree, AttachedPaper. Stubs for Citation and Lean4Result.

progress.details: LlmService with provider registration, switching, sendMessage delegation, SecretStorage API keys. LlmAuthError and LlmRateLimitError. 11 tests passing. VS Code mock system with register.js hooks.
progress.learnings: Use register.js with Module._resolveFilename hook to mock vscode module in tests. Load before tsx in .mocharc.yml.

progress.details: KnowledgeCache with Memento-backed storage, TTL expiry, get/set/clear/isExpired. 15 tests passing.

progress.details: Lean4Service with isAvailable, verify (success/error/timeout), DI for testability. 13 tests.

codebasePatterns: Webview React apps live in webview-ui/ with their own tsconfig.json (ES2020, jsx react-jsx, module ESNext)
esbuild.webview.js bundles webview-ui/src/index.tsx to out/webview/bundle.js in IIFE format
MathResearchPanel in src/webview/panel.ts uses singleton pattern with static createOrShow()
Webview HTML uses nonce-based CSP for scripts, unsafe-inline for styles, webview.cspSource for all resource origins
VS Code CSS variables (--vscode-editor-background, --vscode-editor-foreground, --vscode-button-background) for theme integration
Use dependency injection (ConfigGetter function) instead of importing vscode directly in service classes -- enables unit testing with mocha+tsx without vscode module mocking
Unit tests use mocha with tsx loader: mocha --require tsx src/test/**/*.test.ts
Services are wired in src/services.ts via createServices() which is called from extension.ts activate()
TreeManager in src/dialogue/tree.ts uses Map<string, DialogueTree> for in-memory storage; activePath tracks the current branch as an ordered array of node IDs from root to leaf
Type stubs for cross-story dependencies (Citation in src/knowledge/types.ts, Lean4Result in src/lean4/types.ts) are created as minimal interfaces to be expanded by their owning stories
Use ConfigGetter injection for vscode.workspace.getConfiguration testability
LlmService in src/llm/service.ts uses Map<string, LlmProvider> for provider registry; async generator sendMessage delegates to active provider
LLM types (LlmMessage, LlmOptions, LlmProvider, LlmAuthError, LlmRateLimitError) in src/llm/types.ts
VS Code SecretStorage accessed via LlmService.getApiKey/setApiKey for secure API key management
For mocha on Node 24 with vscode mock: use .mocharc.json with node-option import=./src/test/hooks.mjs (registers custom ESM resolver) + import=tsx/esm; compile first with tsc then run mocha on out/
Use src/test/register.js to mock vscode module in unit tests
